title: Phyllocalyx involucratus
taxonomy: Phyllocalyx involucratus is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Phyllocalyx, which is a group of tropical plants native to Central and South America. The classification of Phyllocalyx involucratus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the division Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Phyllocalyx involucratus is an ongoing area of research in the field of botany.

intro: Phyllocalyx involucratus, also known as "Phyllanthus" or "Chanca Piedra," is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine. The plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as kidney stones and liver disorders.
